typecheck.summary: 3 TypeScript errors in .next/types/ - Next.js 15 async params not awaited in app/api/products/[id]/route.ts and app/products/[id]/page.tsx. The params type must be Promise<{id: string}> and awaited before use.

lint.summary: 3 warnings (no-img-element in ProductCard, BannerCarousel, product detail page) - non-blocking

reviewsSummary.note: Feature reviews not performed due to typecheck failure

blockingIssues.description: TypeScript errors: Next.js 15 requires params to be typed as Promise<{id: string}> and awaited. Both app/api/products/[id]/route.ts and app/products/[id]/page.tsx use synchronous params destructuring ({params}: {params: {id: string}}) which is incompatible with Next.js 15's async params API. The route handler GET function and page component need to await params before accessing id.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: In app/products/[id]/page.tsx: change type to params: Promise<{id: string}> and use 'const {id} = await params'. In app/api/products/[id]/route.ts: same pattern - type params as Promise and await before accessing id.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: In Next.js 15, dynamic route params are async. All page.tsx and route.ts files with [param] segments must type params as Promise<{param: type}> and await them. Example: const { id } = await params;
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: product-detail-page feature typed params synchronously in both page.tsx and route.ts, causing 3 typecheck errors. This is a Next.js 15 breaking change that will affect all future dynamic routes (events/[id], admin routes, etc.).
